bio.name: Rick Wayne
bio.headline: Rick Wayne is a New York-based commercial and documentary director.
bio.long: Rick Wayne has been directing commercial and brand films for more than 15 years for some of the world's most beloved brands. His agency experience as Executive Creative Director on global campaigns makes him a sharper director on set: treatments that read like strategy decks, the authority to defend a vision in a room of stakeholders, and the ability to translate agency feedback into the next take. His career began in Chicago, expanded in Los Angeles, and he now resides in Brooklyn, NY.
bio.subhed: He builds whatever world the story needs.

beliefs: Yes, and. I trained at Second City. Improv taught me that the best creative happens when you build on what's in front of you instead of protecting what's in your head.
I care less about what we are making than why we are making it. Tell me what's at stake for the person watching and I'll build everything from there.
Play is a directing tool, not a reward for finishing the work. A set that feels like a playground is not for fun, it is to find the magic.
The best idea wins, full stop. I've had ACs save a scene and PAs catch what the agency missed. There is no hierarchy for good ideas.
Actors are a craft. Real human emotion is a superpower. Some of the most powerful moments came from someone who had never been on set before.
I forage. I'm always hunting the extra shot, the unexpected angle, the moment nobody planned.
I've been editing as long as I've been directing. When I call a shot, I can already see the final cut in my head.
Brand and humanity are not opposites. The most commercial work I've made is also the most human.
The best work lives at the intersection of strategy, creativity, and craft.
Great films are not directed. They are collaborated into existence.
